types.name: FineTuningJob

fields.name: id
fields.description: The object identifier, which can be referenced in the API endpoints.

fields.name: created_at
fields.description: The Unix timestamp (in seconds) for when the fine-tuning job was created.

fields.name: hyperparameters
fields.description: The hyperparameters used for the fine-tuning job. This value will only be returned when running `supervised` jobs.

fields.name: model
fields.description: The base model that is being fine-tuned.

fields.name: object
fields.description: The object type, which is always "fine_tuning.job".

fields.name: organization_id
fields.description: The organization that owns the fine-tuning job.

fields.name: result_files
fields.description: The compiled results file ID(s) for the fine-tuning job. You can retrieve the results with the [Files API](/docs/api-reference/files/retrieve-contents).

fields.name: status
fields.description: The current status of the fine-tuning job, which can be either `validating_files`, `queued`, `running`, `succeeded`, `failed`, or `cancelled`.

fields.name: training_file
fields.description: The file ID used for training. You can retrieve the training data with the [Files API](/docs/api-reference/files/retrieve-contents).

fields.name: seed
fields.description: The seed used for the fine-tuning job.

types.description: The `fine_tuning.job` object represents a fine-tuning job that has been created through the API.

types.name: FineTuningJobError

fields.name: code
fields.description: A machine-readable error code.

fields.name: message
fields.description: A human-readable error message.

types.description: For fine-tuning jobs that have `failed`, this will contain more information on the cause of the failure.

types.name: FineTuningJobHyperparameters

fields.name: learning_rate_multiplier
fields.description:
Scaling factor for the learning rate. A smaller learning rate may be useful to avoid
overfitting.

fields.name: n_epochs
fields.description:
The number of epochs to train the model for. An epoch refers to one full cycle
through the training dataset.

types.description: The hyperparameters used for the fine-tuning job. This value will only be returned when running `supervised` jobs.

types.name: FineTuningJobHyperparametersBatchSize
types.description:
Number of examples in each batch. A larger batch size means that model parameters
are updated less frequently, but with lower variance.

types.name: FineTuningJobHyperparametersLearningRateMultiplier
types.description:
Scaling factor for the learning rate. A smaller learning rate may be useful to avoid
overfitting.

types.name: FineTuningJobStatus
types.description: The current status of the fine-tuning job, which can be either `validating_files`, `queued`, `running`, `succeeded`, `failed`, or `cancelled`.

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of the integration being enabled for the fine-tuning job

fields.name: wandb
fields.description:
The settings for your integration with Weights and Biases. This payload specifies the project that
metrics will be sent to. Optionally, you can set an explicit display name for your run, add tags
to your run, and set a default entity (team, username, etc) to be associated with your run.

types.name: FineTuningIntegrationWandb

fields.name: project
fields.description: The name of the project that the new run will be created under.

fields.name: tags
fields.description:
A list of tags to be attached to the newly created run. These tags are passed through directly to WandB. Some
default tags are generated by OpenAI: "openai/finetune", "openai/{base-model}", "openai/{ftjob-abcdef}".

types.description:
The settings for your integration with Weights and Biases. This payload specifies the project that
metrics will be sent to. Optionally, you can set an explicit display name for your run, add tags
to your run, and set a default entity (team, username, etc) to be associated with your run.

types.name: FineTuneMethod

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of method. Is either `supervised`, `dpo`, or `reinforcement`.

types.description: The method used for fine-tuning.

types.name: FineTuneMethodType
types.description: The type of method. Is either `supervised`, `dpo`, or `reinforcement`.

types.name: FineTuneSupervisedMethod
types.description: Configuration for the supervised fine-tuning method.

types.name: FineTuneSupervisedHyperparameters

fields.name: batch_size
fields.description: Number of examples in each batch. A larger batch size means that model parameters are updated less frequently, but with lower variance.

fields.name: learning_rate_multiplier
fields.description: Scaling factor for the learning rate. A smaller learning rate may be useful to avoid overfitting.

fields.name: n_epochs
fields.description: The number of epochs to train the model for. An epoch refers to one full cycle through the training dataset.

types.description: The hyperparameters used for the fine-tuning job.

types.name: FineTuneDpoMethod
types.description: Configuration for the DPO fine-tuning method.

types.name: FineTuneDpoHyperparameters

fields.name: beta
fields.description: The beta value for the DPO method. A higher beta value will increase the weight of the penalty between the policy and reference model.

fields.name: batch_size
fields.description: Number of examples in each batch. A larger batch size means that model parameters are updated less frequently, but with lower variance.

fields.name: learning_rate_multiplier
fields.description: Scaling factor for the learning rate. A smaller learning rate may be useful to avoid overfitting.

fields.name: n_epochs
fields.description: The number of epochs to train the model for. An epoch refers to one full cycle through the training dataset.

types.description: The hyperparameters used for the DPO fine-tuning job.

types.name: FineTuneReinforcementMethod

fields.name: grader
fields.description: The grader used for the fine-tuning job.

types.description: Configuration for the reinforcement fine-tuning method.

types.name: FineTuneReinforcementMethodGrader
types.description: The grader used for the fine-tuning job.

types.name: GraderStringCheck

fields.name: type
fields.description: The object type, which is always `string_check`.

fields.name: name
fields.description: The name of the grader.

fields.name: input
fields.description: The input text. This may include template strings.

fields.name: reference
fields.description: The reference text. This may include template strings.

fields.name: operation
fields.description: The string check operation to perform. One of `eq`, `ne`, `like`, or `ilike`.

types.description: A StringCheckGrader object that performs a string comparison between input and reference using a specified operation.

types.name: GraderStringCheckOperation
types.description: The string check operation to perform. One of `eq`, `ne`, `like`, or `ilike`.

types.name: GraderTextSimilarity

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of grader.

fields.name: name
fields.description: The name of the grader.

fields.name: input
fields.description: The text being graded.

fields.name: reference
fields.description: The text being graded against.

fields.name: evaluation_metric
fields.description:
The evaluation metric to use. One of `cosine`, `fuzzy_match`, `bleu`, 
`gleu`, `meteor`, `rouge_1`, `rouge_2`, `rouge_3`, `rouge_4`, `rouge_5`, 
or `rouge_l`.

types.description: A TextSimilarityGrader object which grades text based on similarity metrics.

types.name: GraderTextSimilarityEvaluationMetric
types.description:
The evaluation metric to use. One of `cosine`, `fuzzy_match`, `bleu`, 
`gleu`, `meteor`, `rouge_1`, `rouge_2`, `rouge_3`, `rouge_4`, `rouge_5`, 
or `rouge_l`.

types.name: GraderPython

fields.name: type
fields.description: The object type, which is always `python`.

fields.name: name
fields.description: The name of the grader.

fields.name: source
fields.description: The source code of the python script.

fields.name: image_tag
fields.description: The image tag to use for the python script.

types.description: A PythonGrader object that runs a python script on the input.

types.name: GraderScoreModel

fields.name: type
fields.description: The object type, which is always `score_model`.

fields.name: name
fields.description: The name of the grader.

fields.name: model
fields.description: The model to use for the evaluation.

fields.name: sampling_params
fields.description: The sampling parameters for the model.

fields.name: input
fields.description: The input messages evaluated by the grader. Supports text, output text, input image, and input audio content blocks, and may include template strings.

fields.name: range
fields.description: The range of the score. Defaults to `[0, 1]`.

types.description: A ScoreModelGrader object that uses a model to assign a score to the input.

types.name: GraderScoreModelSamplingParams
types.description: The sampling parameters for the model.

types.name: ReasoningEffort
types.description:
Constrains effort on reasoning for
[reasoning models](https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/reasoning).
Currently supported values are `none`, `minimal`, `low`, `medium`, `high`, and `xhigh`. Reducing
reasoning effort can result in faster responses and fewer tokens used
on reasoning in a response.

- `gpt-5.1` defaults to `none`, which does not perform reasoning. The supported reasoning values for `gpt-5.1` are `none`, `low`, `medium`, and `high`. Tool calls are supported for all reasoning values in gpt-5.1.
- All models before `gpt-5.1` default to `medium` reasoning effort, and do not support `none`.
- The `gpt-5-pro` model defaults to (and only supports) `high` reasoning effort.
- `xhigh` is supported for all models after `gpt-5.1-codex-max`.

types.name: EvalItem

fields.name: role
fields.description:
The role of the message input. One of `user`, `assistant`, `system`, or
`developer`.

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of the message input. Always `message`.

types.description:
A message input to the model with a role indicating instruction following
hierarchy. Instructions given with the `developer` or `system` role take
precedence over instructions given with the `user` role. Messages with the
`assistant` role are presumed to have been generated by the model in previous
interactions.

types.name: EvalItemRole
types.description:
The role of the message input. One of `user`, `assistant`, `system`, or
`developer`.

types.name: EvalItemContent
types.description: Inputs to the model - can contain template strings. Supports text, output text, input images, and input audio, either as a single item or an array of items.

types.name: EvalItemContentItem
types.description: A single content item: input text, output text, input image, or input audio.

types.name: InputTextContent

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of the input item. Always `input_text`.

fields.name: text
fields.description: The text input to the model.

types.description: A text input to the model.

types.name: EvalItemContentOutputText

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of the output text. Always `output_text`.

fields.name: text
fields.description: The text output from the model.

types.description: A text output from the model.

types.name: EvalItemInputImage

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of the image input. Always `input_image`.

fields.name: image_url
fields.description: The URL of the image input.

fields.name: detail
fields.description: The detail level of the image to be sent to the model. One of `high`, `low`, or `auto`. Defaults to `auto`.

types.description: An image input block used within EvalItem content arrays.

types.name: InputAudio

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of the input item. Always `input_audio`.

types.description: An audio input to the model.

fields.name: data
fields.description: Base64-encoded audio data.

fields.name: format
fields.description:
The format of the audio data. Currently supported formats are `mp3` and
`wav`.

types.name: InputAudioInputAudioFormat
types.description:
The format of the audio data. Currently supported formats are `mp3` and
`wav`.

types.name: GraderMulti

fields.name: type
fields.description: The object type, which is always `multi`.

fields.name: name
fields.description: The name of the grader.

fields.name: calculate_output
fields.description: A formula to calculate the output based on grader results.

types.description: A MultiGrader object combines the output of multiple graders to produce a single score.

types.name: GraderLabelModel

fields.name: type
fields.description: The object type, which is always `label_model`.

fields.name: name
fields.description: The name of the grader.

fields.name: model
fields.description: The model to use for the evaluation. Must support structured outputs.

fields.name: labels
fields.description: The labels to assign to each item in the evaluation.

fields.name: passing_labels
fields.description: The labels that indicate a passing result. Must be a subset of labels.

types.description:
A LabelModelGrader object which uses a model to assign labels to each item
in the evaluation.

types.name: FineTuneReinforcementHyperparameters

fields.name: batch_size
fields.description: Number of examples in each batch. A larger batch size means that model parameters are updated less frequently, but with lower variance.

fields.name: learning_rate_multiplier
fields.description: Scaling factor for the learning rate. A smaller learning rate may be useful to avoid overfitting.

fields.name: n_epochs
fields.description: The number of epochs to train the model for. An epoch refers to one full cycle through the training dataset.

fields.name: reasoning_effort
fields.description: Level of reasoning effort.

fields.name: compute_multiplier
fields.description: Multiplier on amount of compute used for exploring search space during training.

fields.name: eval_interval
fields.description: The number of training steps between evaluation runs.

fields.name: eval_samples
fields.description: Number of evaluation samples to generate per training step.

types.description: The hyperparameters used for the reinforcement fine-tuning job.

types.name: FineTuneReinforcementHyperparametersReasoningEffort
types.description: Level of reasoning effort.

pathParams.name: fine_tuning_job_id
pathParams.description: The ID of the fine-tuning job to cancel.
